name: VLDL Cholesterol (VLDL-C)
summary: VLDL cholesterol is the cholesterol carried in very-low-density lipoprotein particles, which transport triglycerides made in the liver. There is no simple direct test for it, so labs typically estimate VLDL as about one-fifth of the triglyceride level.
whatItMeans: Because VLDL is estimated from triglycerides, a higher VLDL value largely mirrors elevated triglycerides and is considered atherogenic — it can contribute to plaque buildup in arteries. Higher values are generally viewed as less favorable, while lower values are preferable. As a derived number it carries the same caveats as triglycerides, including sensitivity to recent food intake.
raises: Elevated triglycerides (the value it is calculated from)
lowers: Lowering triglycerides through diet and weight loss
confounders: VLDL is usually calculated (triglycerides ÷ 5), not measured, so it inherits triglyceride confounders
The triglyceride/5 estimate is unreliable when triglycerides are ≥ 400 mg/dL
Non-fasting samples inflate the underlying triglycerides and therefore VLDL
Recent alcohol intake raises the value

ranges.note: Normal 2–30 mg/dL; > 30 mg/dL is considered high and may increase cardiovascular risk (MedlinePlus). Reference ranges vary slightly by lab.

references.title: VLDL Cholesterol Test: What the Result Means (Testing.com)
